elements.definition: Avoiding or reducing hassles and inconveniences
Reduces complexity and keeps things simple
Connects organizations and users with others
Helps integrate different facets of business
Provides variety of goods/services to choose from
Is stable company for foreseeable future
Allows customer to operate in more locations
Moves beyond standard to allow customization
Makes users more marketable in their field
Is enjoyable to interact with or rewarding
Helps customer anticipate direction of markets
Gives buyers hope for future of their organization
Helps customer be more socially responsible
Inspired by the future and what could be
Have a craving to know more
Can take direction and follow through
Have presence and can take control
Generally find it easy to put thoughts into words
Feel confident in ability to manage own life
Love the challenge of meeting new people
Prefer to go with the flow
Have faith in the links between all things
Recognize and cultivate the potential in others
Can sense the feelings of other people
Intrigued by the unique qualities of each person
Have an enthusiasm that is contagious
How clear and well-articulated is the WHY on the website?
Does copy reflect true beliefs rather than generic marketing?
Does the site inspire and motivate visitors?
Is WHY messaging consistent across pages and sections?
Is the WHY unique and meaningful vs competitors?
Does WHY copy connect emotionally with the audience?
What makes the HOW distinctive in copy?
How clearly defined are methods and processes?
Is HOW described consistently across the site?
Does HOW copy show support for the stated WHY?
Are there proof points demonstrating HOW?
Does copy suggest a durable competitive advantage?
Do offerings align with stated WHY and HOW?
Is quality signaled in WHAT copy?
Does WHAT copy prove value with evidence?
Does copy show innovation while staying on-mission?
Does WHAT copy show fit with market needs?
How clearly is the target audience defined?
Does copy attract people who share the WHY?
Is the audience specific rather than “everyone”?
Does copy show deep understanding of audience needs?
Will the target audience feel the message is for them?
Does copy foster loyalty and advocacy?

elements.categoryName: Ego Archetypes (Leave a Mark on the World)
elements.definition: Represents courage, strength, and the determination to overcome adversity for a noble cause. Heroes inspire hope and serve as role models.

elements.categoryName: Ego Archetypes (Leave a Mark on the World)
elements.definition: Represents transformation, change, and miracles. Represents the ability to turn dreams into reality.

elements.categoryName: Ego Archetypes (Leave a Mark on the World)
elements.definition: Represents an independent spirit that challenges societal norms, rules, and authority. Advocates for change and freedom.
Represents compassion, empathy, and a strong desire to help and care for others.
Represents authority, control, and leadership, appealing to the desire for stability and order.
Represents innovation, artistry, and expression. Values creativity and bringing visions to life.
Embodies qualities of optimism, simplicity, and purity. Represents a desire for honesty and wholesomeness.
Represents freedom, adventure, and discovery. Embodies the spirit of curiosity, independence, and a desire for self-discovery.
Represents wisdom, knowledge, and a deep desire to understand the world and its mysteries. Sages seek truth, always pursuing learning and enlightenment.
Represents the everyday person which is relatable to everyone. Embodies qualities of being down-to-earth, practical, and approachable.
Represents joy, humor, and entertainment. Brings lightness and fun to experiences.
Represents passion, sensuality, and connection, appealing to the desires for intimacy and relationships.
Signals of market demand in site copy or offers
Trending topics and search-intent language on the site
Seasonal or cyclical demand patterns mentioned
Differentiation gaps vs competitors in messaging
Emerging revenue opportunities signaled in content
Upsell paths and tiered offer language
Distinct customer segments addressed on the site
Content marketing gaps and resource coverage
SEO and organic growth opportunity signals
Social media and community trend alignment
